Written by the rotation leader (Mode B, §14.5) and read by every pod on each forwarded SPA request; see §13.21 \"Scoped-key rotation coordination\".\n760\t- `miroir:search_ui_scoped_key_observed:<pod>:<index>` (hash, fields `{generation, observed_at}`) with `EXPIRE` 60s, refreshed on each use of the scoped key. The revocation safety gate reads these keys to decide when every live peer has observed the new generation; see §13.21 \"Revocation safety gate\". The rotation leader-lease itself is already covered by the generic `miroir:lease:<scope>` entry above with scope `search_ui_key_rotation:<index>`.\n761\t- `miroir:admin_session:<session_id>` (hash, fields `{csrf_token, admin_key_hash, created_at, expires_at, revoked, user_agent, source_ip}`) with `EXPIRE` matching `admin_ui.session_ttl_s`. Logout sets `revoked: 1` on the key (kept until `EXPIRE` fires) so every pod's cookie verification re-checks revocation on each request; a Redis Pub/Sub channel `miroir:admin_session:revoked` is additionally notified so in-memory caches can invalidate immediately rather than waiting for the next round-trip.\n762\t- `miroir:ratelimit:searchui:<ip>` (string counter) with `EXPIRE` per `search_ui.rate_limit.redis_ttl_s` (default 60s); bounded by active IP count; see §13.21.\n763\t- `miroir:ratelimit:adminlogin:<ip>` (string counter) with `EXPIRE` 60s; bounded by active attacker IP count. Backs the `POST /_miroir/admin/login` 10/minute-per-IP rate limit when `miroir.replicas > 1`; Helm `values.schema.json` rejects local-only admin-login rate limiting in HA. A companion key `miroir:ratelimit:adminlogin:backoff:<ip>` (hash `{failed_count, next_allowed_at}`) implements the exponential-backoff clause — after 5 consecutive failed attempts the backoff doubles per attempt (10m, 20m, 40m, …) until a valid login resets the counter.\n764\t- `miroir:cdc:overflow:<sink>` (list); bounded by `cdc.buffer.redis_bytes` (default 1 GiB per sink); see §13.13.\n765\t\n766\tTTLs are set on the keyspaces marked above; list-wide queries are served from the `_index` secondary sets so no `SCAN` is required on the hot path. None of the new tables use Redis `EXPIRE` — they are all long-lived configuration or bounded-by-cardinality history.\n767\t\n768\t### Configuration schema\n769\t\n770\t```yaml\n771\tmiroir:\n772\t  master_key: \"\"              # client-facing API key (env: MIROIR_MASTER_KEY)\n773\t  node_master_key: \"\"         # key Miroir uses on nodes (env: MIROIR_NODE_MASTER_KEY)\n774\t  shards: 64\n775\t  replication_factor: 2       # production recommended; Helm chart dev default is 1\n776\t\n777\t  task_store:\n778\t    backend: sqlite            # sqlite | redis\n779\t    path: /data/miroir-tasks.db\n780\t    url: \"\"                    # for redis: redis://host:6379\n781\t\n782\t  admin:\n783\t    enabled: true\n784\t    api_key: \"\"                # env: MIROIR_ADMIN_API_KEY\n785\t\n786\t  # Number of independent query pools. Each group holds all shards.\n787\t  # Writes go to all groups; each query goes to exactly one group.\n788\t  # This sample shows a two-group production topology. The default when unset is 1.\n789\t  replica_groups: 2\n790\t\n791\t  nodes:\n792\t  - id: \"meili-0\"              # stable, unique — never reuse after removal\n793\t    address: \"http://meili-0.search.svc:7700\"\n794\t    replica_group: 0           # which group this node belongs to\n795\t  - id: \"meili-1\"\n796\t    address: \"http://meili-1.search.svc:7700\"\n797\t    replica_group: 0\n798\t  - id: \"meili-2\"\n799\t    address: \"http://meili-2.search.svc:7700\"\n800\t    replica_group: 0\n801\t  - id: \"meili-3\"\n802\t    address: \"http://meili-3.search.svc:7700\"\n803\t    replica_group: 1\n804\t  - id: \"meili-4\"\n805\t    address: \"http://meili-4.search.svc:7700\"\n806\t    replica_group: 1\n807\t  - id: \"meili-5\"\n808\t    address: \"http://meili-5.search.svc:7700\"\n809\t    replica_group: 1\n810\t\n811\t  health:\n812\t    interval_ms: 5000\n813\t    timeout_ms: 2000\n814\t    unhealthy_threshold: 3\n815\t    recovery_threshold: 2\n816\t\n817\t  scatter:\n818\t    node_timeout_ms: 5000\n819\t    retry_on_timeout: true\n820\t    unavailable_shard_policy: partial   # partial | error\n821\t\n822\t  rebalancer:\n823\t    auto_rebalance_on_recovery: true\n824\t    max_concurrent_migrations: 4\n825\t    migration_timeout_s: 3600\n826\t\n827\t  server:\n828\t    port: 7700\n829\t    bind: \"0.0.0.0\"\n830\t    max_body_bytes: 104857600   # 100 MiB\n831\t```\n832\t\n833\t**Note on `scatter.retry_on_timeout`.** When this flag is `true` (the default), Miroir dedups retries **orchestrator-side** — Meilisearch CE has no idempotency layer, and §13 requires unmodified CE nodes. Internally, Miroir maintains a per-target-node retry cache keyed by `sha256(document_batch_bytes || target_node_id || client_idempotency_key_or_mtask_id)`, with the last observed terminal response stored against each key. When a write times out and a retry fires, Miroir first checks whether it has already dispatched the same key to that node; if yes and the node returned a terminal status, Miroir returns the cached response instead of re-dispatching, so no duplicate node-side task is ever created. If the prior dispatch has no recorded terminal status (still in flight or unknown), Miroir resends as normal and reconciles against the node's subsequent task listing. This avoids duplicate writes without any node cooperation. See §13.10 for the same retry cache (it is a single mechanism shared across subsystems) and §14.2 for how this cache's memory budget is allocated.\n834\t\n835\tAll secrets are read from environment variables in production — never baked into config files or images.\n836\t\n837\t### Admin API (`/_miroir/`)\n838\t\n839\tReserved path prefix for management operations, not proxied to nodes. Gated by the admin API key.\n840\t\n841\tAll admin endpoints accept either `Authorization: Bearer <MIROIR_ADMIN_API_KEY>` or `X-Admin-Key: <MIROIR_ADMIN_API_KEY>`. The bearer form is preferred; `X-Admin-Key` is primarily used by the Admin UI (§13.19) for browser fetches where setting the `Authorization` header is inconvenient. Endpoints marked **client-facing** below (e.g. `/multi-search`, `/indexes/{uid}/explain`) use the regular `master_key` rather than the admin key. The `/_miroir/` prefix is cluster-internal only — no ingress rule exposes it publicly.\n842\t\n843\t| Endpoint | Description |\n844\t|----------|-------------|\n845\t| **Topology** | |\n846\t| `GET /_miroir/topology` | Node list, health state, shard assignments |\n847\t| `GET /_miroir/shards` | Shard → node mapping table |\n848\t| `GET /_miroir/ready` | Readiness check (used by K8s readiness probe) |\n849\t| **Nodes** | |\n850\t| `POST /_miroir/nodes` | Add a node (triggers rebalance) |\n851\t| `DELETE /_miroir/nodes/{id}` | Drain and remove a node |\n852\t| `POST /_miroir/nodes/{id}/drain` | Begin draining shards off a node without removing it (see §6 Scaling) |\n853\t| **Rebalance** | |\n854\t| `POST /_miroir/rebalance` | Manually trigger rebalance |\n855\t| `GET /_miroir/rebalance/status` | Current rebalance progress |\n856\t| **Reshard (§13.1)** | |\n857\t| `POST /_miroir/indexes/{uid}/reshard` | Begin online resharding to a new S (body: `{\"new_shards\": N, \"throttle_docs_per_sec\": R}`) |\n858\t| `GET /_miroir/indexes/{uid}/reshard/status` | Current phase and backfill progress |\n859\t| **Aliases (§13.7)** | |\n860\t| `POST /_miroir/aliases` | Create an alias (body: `{\"name\": \"...\", \"target\": \"...\"}`) |\n861\t| `GET /_miroir/aliases` | List all aliases |\n862\t| `GET /_miroir/aliases/{name}` | Current target plus flip history |\n863\t| `PUT /_miroir/aliases/{name}` | Atomically flip an alias to a new target (body: `{\"target\": \"...\"}`) |\n864\t| `DELETE /_miroir/aliases/{name}` | Delete an alias (underlying index untouched) |\n865\t| **Dumps (§13.9)** | |\n866\t| `POST /_miroir/dumps/import` | Streaming dump import (multipart body with the `.dump` file; returns `{\"miroir_task_id\": \"...\"}`) |\n867\t| `GET /_miroir/dumps/import/{id}/status` | Progress and phase of a streaming import job |\n868\t| **Observability** | |\n869\t| `GET /_miroir/metrics` | Prometheus metrics (auth-gated mirror of `:9090/metrics`) |\n870\t| **CDC (§13.13)** | |\n871\t| `GET /_miroir/changes?since={cursor}&index={uid}` | Long-poll change stream for the `internal` queue sink; `cursor` is a monotonic per-index sequence number, `index` is required. Returns a bounded batch plus the next cursor. |\n872\t| **TTL (§13.14)** | |\n873\t| `POST /_miroir/indexes/{uid}/ttl-policy` | Set or update per-index TTL sweep policy. Body: `{\"sweep_interval_s\": N, \"max_deletes_per_sweep\": M, \"enabled\": bool}`. Overrides the global `ttl.per_index_overrides` entry. |\n874\t| **Shadow (§13.16)** | |\n875\t| `GET /_miroir/shadow/diff` | Read the in-memory diff ring buffer. Query: `?target={name}&limit=N&since_id=X&kind={hits,ranking,latency,error}`. Ring buffer size bounded by `shadow.diff_buffer_size`. |\n876\t| **Canaries (§13.18)** | |\n877\t| `POST /_miroir/canaries` | Create or modify a canary definition (body matches the `canaries:` YAML schema in §13.18). |\n878\t| `GET /_miroir/canaries/status` | List canaries with the last N runs each (bounded by `canary_runner.run_history_per_canary`), pass/fail counts, and last-failure detail. |\n879\t| `POST /_miroir/canaries/capture` | Record the next M production queries + responses as golden pairs; body: `{\"index\": \"...\", \"count\": M, \"name_prefix\": \"...\"}`. |\n880\t| **Search UI config (§13.21)** | |\n881\t| `POST /_miroir/ui/search/{index}/config` | Set the per-index search-UI configuration (title, facets, sort options, templates, etc.). Body matches the `search_ui:` entry schema in §13.21. |\n882\t| `GET /_miroir/ui/search/locale/{lang}.json` | Public (no auth) locale fetch for the search UI. Returns translations JSON; served with long `max-age`. This endpoint is the one exception to the \"admin-gated under `/_miroir/`\" rule — it is whitelisted so unauthenticated end-user SPAs can load strings. |\n883\t| **Search UI (§13.21)** | |\n884\t| `GET /_miroir/ui/search/{index}/session` | JWT session-minting entry point for the end-user search UI. Returns `{token, expires_at, index, rate_limit}`. Auth: `public` mode — IP rate-limited (no credentials); `shared_key` — requires `X-Search-UI-Key`; `oauth_proxy` — requires upstream auth headers (e.g. `X-Forwarded-User`, `X-Forwarded-Groups`). See §13.21. |\n885\t| `POST /_miroir/ui/search/{index}/beacon` | Receives client-emitted analytics/latency beacons from the embedded SPA. Body: `{type: \"click_through\" \\| \"latency\", event_id: <uuid>, query: <string>, result_id?: <string>, position?: <int>, duration_ms?: <int>, timestamp: <int ms>}`. Auth: same JWT as other search UI endpoints (`Authorization: Bearer <jwt>` with `beacon` scope — see §13.21 JWT claims). Rate-limited per `search_ui.rate_limit` (shares the `miroir:ratelimit:searchui:<ip>` bucket with queries). Emits a CDC event with `type: click_through` (always emitted) or `type: latency` (subject to `cdc.emit_internal_writes`). `event_id` is used as the dedup key in the idempotency cache (matches the §13.10 idempotency pattern without requiring the `Idempotency-Key` header — `event_id` plays the same role). See §13.21. |\n886\t| `POST /_miroir/ui/search/{index}/rotate-scoped-key` | Manually trigger scoped-key rotation for the search UI (normally automatic `scoped_key_rotate_before_expiry_days` before expiry). Body: `{force: boolean}` — `force=true` bypasses the `scoped_key_rotate_before_expiry_days` timing gate and rotates immediately regardless of how recent the current key is; `force=false` (or omitted) honors the timing gate and is a no-op if the current key is not yet within the rotation window. Auth: admin-gated (`Authorization: Bearer <admin_key>` or `X-Admin-Key`). Response: `{old_key_id, new_key_id, generation, rotated_at}`. See §13.21 \"Scoped-key rotation coordination\". |\n887\t| **Admin UI (§13.19)** | |\n888\t| `GET /_miroir/admin` | Embedded single-page admin UI. Authenticates via admin API key (`Authorization: Bearer` or `X-Admin-Key`), or a session cookie after a login form. Static assets served from `rust-embed`. |\n889\t| `POST /_miroir/admin/login` | Exchange admin credentials for a session cookie. Body: `{admin_key}`. On success sets `Set-Cookie: miroir_admin_session=<sealed>; HttpOnly; Secure; SameSite=Strict` (sealed with `ADMIN_SESSION_SEAL_KEY` — see §9) and returns `{csrf_token}`; the session row is persisted to `admin_sessions` so logout on any pod is visible to all pods. Rate limit 10/minute per source IP, backed by `miroir:ratelimit:adminlogin:<ip>` when `miroir.replicas > 1` (required — Helm `values.schema.json` rejects local-only admin login rate-limiting in HA). Failed-login exponential backoff: after 5 consecutive failures from the same IP, the backoff window doubles per attempt (10m, 20m, 40m, …) up to a 24h cap, tracked in `miroir:ratelimit:adminlogin:backoff:<ip>`; a successful login resets both counters. |\n890\t| `POST /_miroir/admin/logout` | Invalidates the session cookie server-side (revokes the sealed session ID) and clears it client-side (`Set-Cookie: miroir_admin_session=; Max-Age=0`). |\n891\t| **Public UI (§13.21)** | |\n892\t| `GET /ui/search/{index}` | Embedded single-page search UI for end users. Auth model configurable per deployment via `search_ui.auth.mode` — see §13.21. Rate-limited per source IP. This is the one public SPA entry point served outside `/_miroir/`. |\n893\t\n894\t**Client API extensions.** Two client-facing additions sit alongside the admin table above but are **not** admin-gated — they use the normal `master_key` authorization:\n895\t\n896\t- `POST /multi-search` — batch query API (§13.11). Body: `{\"queries\": [ {indexUid, q, ...}, ... ]}`. Each query is scattered independently and returned in input order.\n897\t- `POST /indexes/{uid}/explain` — query plan API (§13.20). Same body as `/search`. Returns the orchestrator's resolved plan without executing; `?execute=true` additionally runs the plan and returns the real result.\n898\t\n899\tBoth endpoints are listed here for discoverability; their routing behavior is summarized in the §5 \"Routing by operation type\" buckets.\n900\t\n901\t### Rebalancer\n902\t\n903\tRuns as a background Tokio task. Only one Miroir instance runs the rebalancer at a time (advisory lock in SQLite/Redis).\n904\t\n905\tThe rebalancer relies on `_miroir_shard` being a filterable attribute on every node (set at index creation). This allows targeted retrieval of exactly the documents belonging to a given shard without scanning the entire node index.\n906\t\n907\t**Migration flow for node addition:**\n908\t1. Compute `affected_shards`: shards where the new node enters the top-RF (expected count: `~S/(N+1)`)\n909\t2. Begin dual-write: route new inbound writes for affected shards to both old owner and new node\n910\t3. For each affected shard, paginate source node using the shard filter — no full scan required:\n911\t   ```\n912\t   GET /indexes/{uid}/documents?filter=_miroir_shard={shard_id}&limit=1000&offset=0\n913\t   GET /indexes/{uid}/documents?filter=_miroir_shard={shard_id}&limit=1000&offset=1000\n914\t   ... (until exhausted)\n915\t   ```\n916\t4. Write each page to the new node (documents already carry `_miroir_shard`)\n917\t5. Once all pages transferred, mark node `active` for those shards\n918\t6. Stop dual-write; delete the migrated shard from the old node:\n919\t   ```\n920\t   POST /indexes/{uid}/documents/delete  {\"filter\": \"_miroir_shard = {shard_id}\"}\n921\t   ```\n922\t7. Documents on unaffected shards are never read, written, or touched\n923\t\n924\t**Performance**: with S=64 shards, N=3 nodes, adding node-3 migrates `~16` shards. Each migration reads only that shard's documents (roughly `total_docs / 64` documents per shard), not the full node corpus. Total data moved is approximately `total_docs / 4` — the theoretical optimum for a 4-node cluster.\n925\t\n926\t---\n927\t\n928\t## 5. API Surface and Compatibility\n929\t\n930\tMiroir exposes the Meilisearch REST API verbatim. All request and response shapes, status codes, error formats, and headers must match the Meilisearch spec exactly.\n931\t\n932\t### Routing by operation type\n933\t\n934\t**Broadcast to all nodes:**\n935\t- `POST /indexes` — create index\n936\t- `PATCH /indexes/{uid}` — update index settings\n937\t- `DELETE /indexes/{uid}` — delete index\n938\t- All `POST /indexes/{uid}/settings/*` — settings changes\n939\t- `DELETE /indexes/{uid}/documents` with filter body\n940\t- `POST /keys`, `PATCH /keys/{key}`, `DELETE /keys/{key}`\n941\t\n942\t**Shard-routed writes:**\n943\t- `POST /indexes/{uid}/documents` — route each doc by hash(pk) to RF nodes\n944\t- `PUT /indexes/{uid}/documents` — same\n945\t- `DELETE /indexes/{uid}/documents/{id}` — route to owning shard RF nodes\n946\t- `DELETE /indexes/{uid}/documents` by IDs array — route each ID to its shard\n947\t\n948\t**Scatter-gather reads:**\n949\t- `POST /indexes/{uid}/search` — fan out to covering set, merge results"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"f09bdd72-4cc9-4abf-a3ea-8447c2bce56a","uuid":"fa866a3d-3dbe-4d44-a440-e4ccd4843e86","timestamp":"2026-05-23T16:52:06.406Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/home/coding/miroir/docs/plan/plan.md","content":"- `miroir:idemp:<key>` (hash) with `EXPIRE` matching `idempotency.ttl_seconds`.\n- `miroir:jobs:<id>` (hash); queue-depth set `miroir:jobs:_queued` feeds the HPA signal of §14.5.\n- `miroir:lease:<scope>` (string, `SET NX EX 10` renewed every 3s) per §14.5 Mode B.\n- `miroir:canary:<id>` (hash); set `miroir:canary:_index` backs `GET /_miroir/canaries/status`.\n- `miroir:canary_runs:<canary_id>` (sorted set keyed by `ran_at`, value = JSON row); `ZREMRANGEBYRANK` trims to `canary_runner.run_history_per_canary`.\n- `miroir:cdc_cursor:<sink>:<index>` (string, integer seq).\n- `miroir:tenant_map:<sha256_key>` (hash); no secondary index — lookups are always by exact key hash.\n- `miroir:rollover:<name>` (hash); set `miroir:rollover:_index` backs the ILM leader's scan.\n- `miroir:search_ui_config:<index>` (hash).\n- `miroir:search_ui_scoped_key:<index>` (hash, fields `{primary_uid, previous_uid, rotated_at, generation}`); no automatic TTL — state is long-lived until the next rotation. Written by the rotation leader (Mode B, §14.5) and read by every pod on each forwarded SPA request; see §13.21 \"Scoped-key rotation coordination\".\n- `miroir:search_ui_scoped_key_observed:<pod>:<index>` (hash, fields `{generation, observed_at}`) with `EXPIRE` 60s, refreshed on each use of the scoped key. The revocation safety gate reads these keys to decide when every live peer has observed the new generation; see §13.21 \"Revocation safety gate\". The rotation leader-lease itself is already covered by the generic `miroir:lease:<scope>` entry above with scope `search_ui_key_rotation:<index>`.\n- `miroir:admin_session:<session_id>` (hash, fields `{csrf_token, admin_key_hash, created_at, expires_at, revoked, user_agent, source_ip}`) with `EXPIRE` matching `admin_ui.session_ttl_s`. Logout sets `revoked: 1` on the key (kept until `EXPIRE` fires) so every pod's cookie verification re-checks revocation on each request; a Redis Pub/Sub channel `miroir:admin_session:revoked` is additionally notified so in-memory caches can invalidate immediately rather than waiting for the next round-trip.\n- `miroir:ratelimit:searchui:<ip>` (string counter) with `EXPIRE` per `search_ui.rate_limit.redis_ttl_s` (default 60s); bounded by active IP count; see §13.21.\n- `miroir:ratelimit:adminlogin:<ip>` (string counter) with `EXPIRE` 60s; bounded by active attacker IP count. Backs the `POST /_miroir/admin/login` 10/minute-per-IP rate limit when `miroir.replicas > 1`; Helm `values.schema.json` rejects local-only admin-login rate limiting in HA. A companion key `miroir:ratelimit:adminlogin:backoff:<ip>` (hash `{failed_count, next_allowed_at}`) implements the exponential-backoff clause — after 5 consecutive failed attempts the backoff doubles per attempt (10m, 20m, 40m, …) until a valid login resets the counter.\n- `miroir:cdc:overflow:<sink>` (list); bounded by `cdc.buffer.redis_bytes` (default 1 GiB per sink); see §13.13.\n\nTTLs are set on the keyspaces marked above; list-wide queries are served from the `_index` secondary sets so no `SCAN` is required on the hot path. None of the new tables use Redis `EXPIRE` — they are all long-lived configuration or bounded-by-cardinality history.\n\n### Configuration schema\n\n```yaml\nmiroir:\n  master_key: \"\"              # client-facing API key (env: MIROIR_MASTER_KEY)\n  node_master_key: \"\"         # key Miroir uses on nodes (env: MIROIR_NODE_MASTER_KEY)\n  shards: 64\n  replication_factor: 2       # production recommended; Helm chart dev default is 1\n\n  task_store:\n    backend: sqlite            # sqlite | redis\n    path: /data/miroir-tasks.db\n    url: \"\"                    # for redis: redis://host:6379\n\n  admin:\n    enabled: true\n    api_key: \"\"                # env: MIROIR_ADMIN_API_KEY\n\n  # Number of independent query pools. Each group holds all shards.\n  # Writes go to all groups; each query goes to exactly one group.\n  # This sample shows a two-group production topology. The default when unset is 1.\n  replica_groups: 2\n\n  nodes:\n  - id: \"meili-0\"              # stable, unique — never reuse after removal\n    address: \"http://meili-0.search.svc:7700\"\n    replica_group: 0           # which group this node belongs to\n  - id: \"meili-1\"\n    address: \"http://meili-1.search.svc:7700\"\n    replica_group: 0\n  - id: \"meili-2\"\n    address: \"http://meili-2.search.svc:7700\"\n    replica_group: 0\n  - id: \"meili-3\"\n    address: \"http://meili-3.search.svc:7700\"\n    replica_group: 1\n  - id: \"meili-4\"\n    address: \"http://meili-4.search.svc:7700\"\n    replica_group: 1\n  - id: \"meili-5\"\n    address: \"http://meili-5.search.svc:7700\"\n    replica_group: 1\n\n  health:\n    interval_ms: 5000\n    timeout_ms: 2000\n    unhealthy_threshold: 3\n    recovery_threshold: 2\n\n  scatter:\n    node_timeout_ms: 5000\n    retry_on_timeout: true\n    unavailable_shard_policy: partial   # partial | error\n\n  rebalancer:\n    auto_rebalance_on_recovery: true\n    max_concurrent_migrations: 4\n    migration_timeout_s: 3600\n\n  server:\n    port: 7700\n    bind: \"0.0.0.0\"\n    max_body_bytes: 104857600   # 100 MiB\n```\n\n**Note on `scatter.retry_on_timeout`.** When this flag is `true` (the default), Miroir dedups retries **orchestrator-side** — Meilisearch CE has no idempotency layer, and §13 requires unmodified CE nodes. Internally, Miroir maintains a per-target-node retry cache keyed by `sha256(document_batch_bytes || target_node_id || client_idempotency_key_or_mtask_id)`, with the last observed terminal response stored against each key. When a write times out and a retry fires, Miroir first checks whether it has already dispatched the same key to that node; if yes and the node returned a terminal status, Miroir returns the cached response instead of re-dispatching, so no duplicate node-side task is ever created. If the prior dispatch has no recorded terminal status (still in flight or unknown), Miroir resends as normal and reconciles against the node's subsequent task listing. This avoids duplicate writes without any node cooperation. See §13.10 for the same retry cache (it is a single mechanism shared across subsystems) and §14.2 for how this cache's memory budget is allocated.\n\nAll secrets are read from environment variables in production — never baked into config files or images.\n\n### Admin API (`/_miroir/`)\n\nReserved path prefix for management operations, not proxied to nodes. Gated by the admin API key.\n\nAll admin endpoints accept either `Authorization: Bearer <MIROIR_ADMIN_API_KEY>` or `X-Admin-Key: <MIROIR_ADMIN_API_KEY>`. The bearer form is preferred; `X-Admin-Key` is primarily used by the Admin UI (§13.19) for browser fetches where setting the `Authorization` header is inconvenient. Endpoints marked **client-facing** below (e.g. `/multi-search`, `/indexes/{uid}/explain`) use the regular `master_key` rather than the admin key. The `/_miroir/` prefix is cluster-internal only — no ingress rule exposes it publicly.\n\n| Endpoint | Description |\n|----------|-------------|\n| **Topology** | |\n| `GET /_miroir/topology` | Node list, health state, shard assignments |\n| `GET /_miroir/shards` | Shard → node mapping table |\n| `GET /_miroir/ready` | Readiness check (used by K8s readiness probe) |\n| **Nodes** | |\n| `POST /_miroir/nodes` | Add a node (triggers rebalance) |\n| `DELETE /_miroir/nodes/{id}` | Drain and remove a node |\n| `POST /_miroir/nodes/{id}/drain` | Begin draining shards off a node without removing it (see §6 Scaling) |\n| **Rebalance** | |\n| `POST /_miroir/rebalance` | Manually trigger rebalance |\n| `GET /_miroir/rebalance/status` | Current rebalance progress |\n| **Reshard (§13.1)** | |\n| `POST /_miroir/indexes/{uid}/reshard` | Begin online resharding to a new S (body: `{\"new_shards\": N, \"throttle_docs_per_sec\": R}`) |\n| `GET /_miroir/indexes/{uid}/reshard/status` | Current phase and backfill progress |\n| **Aliases (§13.7)** | |\n| `POST /_miroir/aliases` | Create an alias (body: `{\"name\": \"...\", \"target\": \"...\"}`) |\n| `GET /_miroir/aliases` | List all aliases |\n| `GET /_miroir/aliases/{name}` | Current target plus flip history |\n| `PUT /_miroir/aliases/{name}` | Atomically flip an alias to a new target (body: `{\"target\": \"...\"}`) |\n| `DELETE /_miroir/aliases/{name}` | Delete an alias (underlying index untouched) |\n| **Dumps (§13.9)** | |\n| `POST /_miroir/dumps/import` | Streaming dump import (multipart body with the `.dump` file; returns `{\"miroir_task_id\": \"...\"}`) |\n| `GET /_miroir/dumps/import/{id}/status` | Progress and phase of a streaming import job |\n| **Observability** | |\n| `GET /_miroir/metrics` | Prometheus metrics (auth-gated mirror of `:9090/metrics`) |\n| **CDC (§13.13)** | |\n| `GET /_miroir/changes?since={cursor}&index={uid}` | Long-poll change stream for the `internal` queue sink; `cursor` is a monotonic per-index sequence number, `index` is required. Returns a bounded batch plus the next cursor. |\n| **TTL (§13.14)** | |\n| `POST /_miroir/indexes/{uid}/ttl-policy` | Set or update per-index TTL sweep policy. Body: `{\"sweep_interval_s\": N, \"max_deletes_per_sweep\": M, \"enabled\": bool}`. Overrides the global `ttl.per_index_overrides` entry. |\n| **Shadow (§13.16)** | |\n| `GET /_miroir/shadow/diff` | Read the in-memory diff ring buffer. Query: `?target={name}&limit=N&since_id=X&kind={hits,ranking,latency,error}`. Ring buffer size bounded by `shadow.diff_buffer_size`. |\n| **Canaries (§13.18)** | |\n| `POST /_miroir/canaries` | Create or modify a canary definition (body matches the `canaries:` YAML schema in §13.18). |\n| `GET /_miroir/canaries/status` | List canaries with the last N runs each (bounded by `canary_runner.run_history_per_canary`), pass/fail counts, and last-failure detail. |\n| `POST /_miroir/canaries/capture` | Record the next M production queries + responses as golden pairs; body: `{\"index\": \"...\", \"count\": M, \"name_prefix\": \"...\"}`. |\n| **Search UI config (§13.21)** | |\n| `POST /_miroir/ui/search/{index}/config` | Set the per-index search-UI configuration (title, facets, sort options, templates, etc.). Body matches the `search_ui:` entry schema in §13.21. |\n| `GET /_miroir/ui/search/locale/{lang}.json` | Public (no auth) locale fetch for the search UI. Returns translations JSON; served with long `max-age`. This endpoint is the one exception to the \"admin-gated under `/_miroir/`\" rule — it is whitelisted so unauthenticated end-user SPAs can load strings. |\n| **Search UI (§13.21)** | |\n| `GET /_miroir/ui/search/{index}/session` | JWT session-minting entry point for the end-user search UI. Returns `{token, expires_at, index, rate_limit}`. Auth: `public` mode — IP rate-limited (no credentials); `shared_key` — requires `X-Search-UI-Key`; `oauth_proxy` — requires upstream auth headers (e.g. `X-Forwarded-User`, `X-Forwarded-Groups`). See §13.21. |\n| `POST /_miroir/ui/search/{index}/beacon` | Receives client-emitted analytics/latency beacons from the embedded SPA. Body: `{type: \"click_through\" \\| \"latency\", event_id: <uuid>, query: <string>, result_id?: <string>, position?: <int>, duration_ms?: <int>, timestamp: <int ms>}`. Auth: same JWT as other search UI endpoints (`Authorization: Bearer <jwt>` with `beacon` scope — see §13.21 JWT claims). Rate-limited per `search_ui.rate_limit` (shares the `miroir:ratelimit:searchui:<ip>` bucket with queries). Emits a CDC event with `type: click_through` (always emitted) or `type: latency` (subject to `cdc.emit_internal_writes`). `event_id` is used as the dedup key in the idempotency cache (matches the §13.10 idempotency pattern without requiring the `Idempotency-Key` header — `event_id` plays the same role). See §13.21. |\n| `POST /_miroir/ui/search/{index}/rotate-scoped-key` | Manually trigger scoped-key rotation for the search UI (normally automatic `scoped_key_rotate_before_expiry_days` before expiry). Body: `{force: boolean}` — `force=true` bypasses the `scoped_key_rotate_before_expiry_days` timing gate and rotates immediately regardless of how recent the current key is; `force=false` (or omitted) honors the timing gate and is a no-op if the current key is not yet within the rotation window. Auth: admin-gated (`Authorization: Bearer <admin_key>` or `X-Admin-Key`). Response: `{old_key_id, new_key_id, generation, rotated_at}`. See §13.21 \"Scoped-key rotation coordination\". |\n| **Admin UI (§13.19)** | |\n| `GET /_miroir/admin` | Embedded single-page admin UI. Authenticates via admin API key (`Authorization: Bearer` or `X-Admin-Key`), or a session cookie after a login form. Static assets served from `rust-embed`. |\n| `POST /_miroir/admin/login` | Exchange admin credentials for a session cookie. Body: `{admin_key}`. On success sets `Set-Cookie: miroir_admin_session=<sealed>; HttpOnly; Secure; SameSite=Strict` (sealed with `ADMIN_SESSION_SEAL_KEY` — see §9) and returns `{csrf_token}`; the session row is persisted to `admin_sessions` so logout on any pod is visible to all pods. Rate limit 10/minute per source IP, backed by `miroir:ratelimit:adminlogin:<ip>` when `miroir.replicas > 1` (required — Helm `values.schema.json` rejects local-only admin login rate-limiting in HA). Failed-login exponential backoff: after 5 consecutive failures from the same IP, the backoff window doubles per attempt (10m, 20m, 40m, …) up to a 24h cap, tracked in `miroir:ratelimit:adminlogin:backoff:<ip>`; a successful login resets both counters. |\n| `POST /_miroir/admin/logout` | Invalidates the session cookie server-side (revokes the sealed session ID) and clears it client-side (`Set-Cookie: miroir_admin_session=; Max-Age=0`). |\n| **Public UI (§13.21)** | |\n| `GET /ui/search/{index}` | Embedded single-page search UI for end users. Auth model configurable per deployment via `search_ui.auth.mode` — see §13.21. Rate-limited per source IP. This is the one public SPA entry point served outside `/_miroir/`. |\n\n**Client API extensions.** Two client-facing additions sit alongside the admin table above but are **not** admin-gated — they use the normal `master_key` authorization:\n\n- `POST /multi-search` — batch query API (§13.11). Body: `{\"queries\": [ {indexUid, q, ...}, ... ]}`. Each query is scattered independently and returned in input order.\n- `POST /indexes/{uid}/explain` — query plan API (§13.20). Same body as `/search`. Returns the orchestrator's resolved plan without executing; `?execute=true` additionally runs the plan and returns the real result.\n\nBoth endpoints are listed here for discoverability; their routing behavior is summarized in the §5 \"Routing by operation type\" buckets.\n\n### Rebalancer\n\nRuns as a background Tokio task. Only one Miroir instance runs the rebalancer at a time (advisory lock in SQLite/Redis).\n\nThe rebalancer relies on `_miroir_shard` being a filterable attribute on every node (set at index creation). This allows targeted retrieval of exactly the documents belonging to a given shard without scanning the entire node index.\n\n**Migration flow for node addition:**\n1. Compute `affected_shards`: shards where the new node enters the top-RF (expected count: `~S/(N+1)`)\n2. Begin dual-write: route new inbound writes for affected shards to both old owner and new node\n3. For each affected shard, paginate source node using the shard filter — no full scan required:\n   ```\n   GET /indexes/{uid}/documents?filter=_miroir_shard={shard_id}&limit=1000&offset=0\n   GET /indexes/{uid}/documents?filter=_miroir_shard={shard_id}&limit=1000&offset=1000\n   ... (until exhausted)\n   ```\n4. Write each page to the new node (documents already carry `_miroir_shard`)\n5. Once all pages transferred, mark node `active` for those shards\n6. Stop dual-write; delete the migrated shard from the old node:\n   ```\n   POST /indexes/{uid}/documents/delete  {\"filter\": \"_miroir_shard = {shard_id}\"}\n   ```\n7. Documents on unaffected shards are never read, written, or touched\n\n**Performance**: with S=64 shards, N=3 nodes, adding node-3 migrates `~16` shards. Each migration reads only that shard's documents (roughly `total_docs / 64` documents per shard), not the full node corpus. Total data moved is approximately `total_docs / 4` — the theoretical optimum for a 4-node cluster.\n\n---\n\n## 5. API Surface and Compatibility\n\nMiroir exposes the Meilisearch REST API verbatim. All request and response shapes, status codes, error formats, and headers must match the Meilisearch spec exactly.\n\n### Routing by operation type\n\n**Broadcast to all nodes:**\n- `POST /indexes` — create index\n- `PATCH /indexes/{uid}` — update index settings\n- `DELETE /indexes/{uid}` — delete index\n- All `POST /indexes/{uid}/settings/*` — settings changes\n- `DELETE /indexes/{uid}/documents` with filter body\n- `POST /keys`, `PATCH /keys/{key}`, `DELETE /keys/{key}`\n\n**Shard-routed writes:**\n- `POST /indexes/{uid}/documents` — route each doc by hash(pk) to RF nodes\n- `PUT /indexes/{uid}/documents` — same\n- `DELETE /indexes/{uid}/documents/{id}` — route to owning shard RF nodes\n- `DELETE /indexes/{uid}/documents` by IDs array — route each ID to its shard\n\n**Scatter-gather reads:**\n- `POST /indexes/{uid}/search` — fan out to covering set, merge results","numLines":200,"startLine":750,"totalLines":3756}}}

     miroir_core::config::UnavailableShardPolicy::Partial, &strategy,\n317\t    ).await.unwrap();\n318\t\n319\t    // Page 2: offset=5, limit=5 (different query_seq to get different covering set)\n320\t    let plan2 = plan_search_scatter(&topo, 1, 3, shards);\n321\t    // Re-use same mock responses since the node set is the same for this topology\n322\t    let req2 = SearchRequest {\n323\t        index_uid: \"test\".to_string(),\n324\t        query: Some(\"test\".to_string()),\n325\t        offset: 5,\n326\t        limit: 5,\n327\t        filter: None,\n328\t        facets: None,\n329\t        ranking_score: true,\n330\t        body: json!({}),\n331\t        global_idf: None,\n332\t    };\n333\t    let result2 = dfs_query_then_fetch_search(\n334\t        plan2, &mock, req2, &topo,\n335\t        miroir_core::config::UnavailableShardPolicy::Partial, &strategy,\n336\t    ).await.unwrap();\n337\t\n338\t    // Pages should not overlap\n339\t    let page1_ids: std::collections::HashSet<String> = result1.hits.iter()\n340\t        .filter_map(|h| h[\"id\"].as_str().map(|s| s.to_string()))\n341\t        .collect();\n342\t    let page2_ids: std::collections::HashSet<String> = result2.hits.iter()\n343\t        .filter_map(|h| h[\"id\"].as_str().map(|s| s.to_string()))\n344\t        .collect();\n345\t\n346\t    let overlap: std::collections::HashSet<_> = page1_ids.intersection(&page2_ids).collect();\n347\t    assert!(overlap.is_empty(), \"pages should not overlap, but found: {:?}\", overlap);\n348\t\n349\t    // Combined should have 10 hits total (5 per page)\n350\t    assert_eq!(result1.hits.len(), 5, \"page 1 should have 5 hits\");\n351\t    assert_eq!(result2.hits.len(), 5, \"page 2 should have 5 hits\");\n352\t\n353\t    // Verify global ordering: page 1 scores >= page 2 scores\n354\t    let page1_max_score = result1.hits.last()\n355\t        .and_then(|h| h[\"_rankingScore\"].as_f64())\n356\t        .unwrap_or(0.0);\n357\t    let page2_min_score = result2.hits.first()\n358\t        .and_then(|h| h[\"_rankingScore\"].as_f64())\n359\t        .unwrap_or(1.0);\n360\t    assert!(\n361\t        page1_max_score >= page2_min_score,\n362\t        \"page 1 min score ({}) should be >= page 2 max score ({})\",\n363\t        page1_max_score, page2_min_score\n364\t    );\n365\t}\n366\t\n367\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n368\t// DoD 5: write with one group completely down still succeeds + X-Miroir-Degraded\n369\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n370\t\n371\t#[test]\n372\tfn test_degraded_write_one_group_down() {\n373\t    let shards = 4u32;\n374\t    let mut topo = two_group_topology(shards);\n375\t\n376\t    // Take down all nodes in group 1\n377\t    for i in 2..4u32 {\n378\t        topo.node_mut(&NodeId::new(format!(\"node-{i}\")))\n379\t            .unwrap()\n380\t            .transition_to(NodeStatus::Failed)\n381\t            .unwrap();\n382\t    }\n383\t\n384\t    // Verify group 1 nodes are not healthy\n385\t    let node_map = topo.node_map();\n386\t    for group in topo.groups() {\n387\t        if group.id == 1 {\n388\t            let healthy = group.healthy_nodes(&node_map);\n389\t            assert!(healthy.is_empty(), \"group 1 should have no healthy nodes\");\n390\t        }\n391\t    }\n392\t\n393\t    // For each shard, verify at least one group can still accept writes\n394\t    for shard_id in 0..shards {\n395\t        let targets = write_targets(shard_id, &topo);\n396\t        let group0_targets: Vec<_> = targets.iter()\n397\t            .filter(|node_id| {\n398\t                topo.node(node_id).map(|n| n.replica_group == 0).unwrap_or(false)\n399\t            })\n400\t            .collect();\n401\t        assert!(!group0_targets.is_empty(), \"shard {} should have group 0 targets\", shard_id);\n402\t    }\n403\t}\n404\t\n405\t#[test]\n406\tfn test_quorum_logic_group_down() {\n407\t    // Simulate: 2 groups, RF=2. Group 1 is completely down.\n408\t    // Quorum per group = floor(2/2) + 1 = 2.\n409\t    // Group 0: 2 of 2 nodes succeed → quorum met.\n410\t    // Group 1: 0 of 2 nodes succeed → quorum not met.\n411\t    // Overall: at least one group met quorum → write succeeds, degraded header.\n412\t\n413\t    let rf = 2usize;\n414\t    let replica_group_count = 2u32;\n415\t    let quorum_per_group = (rf / 2) + 1; // = 2\n416\t\n417\t    // Group 0: 2 ACKs\n418\t    let group0_acks = 2usize;\n419\t    assert!(group0_acks >= quorum_per_group, \"group 0 met quorum\");\n420\t\n421\t    // Group 1: 0 ACKs\n422\t    let group1_acks = 0usize;\n423\t    assert!(group1_acks < quorum_per_group, \"group 1 missed quorum\");\n424\t\n425\t    // At least one group met quorum → write should succeed\n426\t    let quorum_groups = (group0_acks >= quorum_per_group) as usize\n427\t        + (group1_acks >= quorum_per_group) as usize;\n428\t    assert!(quorum_groups >= 1, \"at least one group met quorum\");\n429\t\n430\t    // Degraded header should be set because group 1 missed quorum\n431\t    let degraded_groups = replica_group_count - quorum_groups as u32;\n432\t    assert_eq!(degraded_groups, 1, \"one group is degraded\");\n433\t}\n434\t\n435\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n436\t// DoD 6: error-format parity with Meilisearch\n437\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n438\t\n439\t#[test]\n440\tfn test_error_shape_byte_for_byte_parity() {\n441\t    // Every MiroirCode serde_json::from_str(&json_str).unwrap();\n447\t\n448\t        // Must have all four fields\n449\t        assert!(parsed.get(\"message\").is_some(), \"{:?}: missing message\", code);\n450\t Phase 2 DoD integration tests.\n//!\n//! Tests covering all Definition of Done criteria:\n//! - 1000 documents indexed across 3 nodes, each retrievable by ID\n//! - Unique-keyword search finds every doc exactly once\n//! - Facet aggregation across 3 color values sums correctly\n//! - Offset/limit paging preserves global ordering\n//! - Write with one group completely down still succeeds + X-Miroir-Degraded\n//! - Error-format parity: every error matches Meilisearch shape\n//! - GET /_miroir/topology matches plan §10 shape\n\nuse miroir_core::api_error::{ErrorType, MeilisearchError, MiroirCode};\nuse miroir_core::merger::ScoreMergeStrategy;\nuse miroir_core::router::{shard_for_key, write_targets};\nuse miroir_core::scatter::{\n    MockNodeClient, SearchRequest,\n    dfs_query_then_fetch_search, plan_search_scatter,\n};\nuse miroir_core::topology::{Node, NodeId, NodeStatus, Topology};\nuse serde_json::{json, Value};\nuse std::collections::HashMap;\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Helper: build a 3-node, 1 replica group topology\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\nfn three_node_topology(shards: u32) -> Topology {\n    let mut topo = Topology::new(shards, 1, 3);\n    for i in 0..3u32 {\n        let node = Node::new(\n            NodeId::new(format!(\"node-{i}\")),\n            format!(\"http://localhost:810{i}\"),\n            0,\n        );\n        topo.add_node(node);\n        topo.node_mut(&NodeId::new(format!(\"node-{i}\")))\n            .unwrap()\n            .transition_to(NodeStatus::Active)\n            .unwrap();\n    }\n    topo\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Helper: build a 2-group, 2-node-per-group topology (4 nodes)\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\nfn two_group_topology(shards: u32) -> Topology {\n    let mut topo = Topology::new(shards, 2, 2);\n    for i in 0..2u32 {\n        let node = Node::new(\n            NodeId::new(format!(\"node-{i}\")),\n            format!(\"http://localhost:810{i}\"),\n            0,\n        );\n        topo.add_node(node);\n        topo.node_mut(&NodeId::new(format!(\"node-{i}\")))\n            .unwrap()\n            .transition_to(NodeStatus::Active)\n            .unwrap();\n    }\n    for i in 2..4u32 {\n        let node = Node::new(\n            NodeId::new(format!(\"node-{i}\")),\n            format!(\"http://localhost:810{i}\"),\n            1,\n        );\n        topo.add_node(node);\n        topo.node_mut(&NodeId::new(format!(\"node-{i}\")))\n            .unwrap()\n            .transition_to(NodeStatus::Active)\n            .unwrap();\n    }\n    topo\n}\n\n// All MiroirCode variants for iteration\nconst ALL_CODES: [MiroirCode; 10] = [\n    MiroirCode::PrimaryKeyRequired,\n    MiroirCode::NoQuorum,\n    MiroirCode::ShardUnavailable,\n    MiroirCode::ReservedField,\n    MiroirCode::IdempotencyKeyReused,\n    MiroirCode::SettingsVersionStale,\n    MiroirCode::MultiAliasNotWritable,\n    MiroirCode::JwtInvalid,\n    MiroirCode::JwtScopeDenied,\n    MiroirCode::InvalidAuth,\n];\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// DoD 1: 1000 documents indexed across 3 nodes, each retrievable by ID\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n#[test]\nfn test_1000_docs_shard_assignment_coverage() {\n    let shards = 8u32;\n    let topo = three_node_topology(shards);\n\n    let mut shard_counts: HashMap<u32, Vec<String>> = HashMap::new();\n    for i in 0..1000u32 {\n        let pk = format!(\"doc-{i}\");\n        let shard = shard_for_key(&pk, shards);\n        shard_counts.entry(shard).or_default().push(pk);\n    }\n\n    // Every shard should have at least one document\n    assert_eq!(shard_counts.len(), shards as usize, \"all shards should receive documents\");\n\n    // All write targets should be reachable\n    for shard_id in 0..shards {\n        let targets = write_targets(shard_id, &topo);\n        assert_eq!(targets.len(), 3, \"RF=3 means 3 write targets per shard\");\n        for node_id in &targets {\n            assert!(topo.node(node_id).is_some(), \"target node {} should exist\", node_id);\n        }\n    }\n\n    // Total count should be 1000\n    let total: usize = shard_counts.values().map(|v| v.len()).sum();\n    assert_eq!(total, 1000);\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// DoD 2: unique-keyword search finds every doc exactly once\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn test_unique_keyword_search_deduplication() {\n    let shards = 4u32;\n    let topo = two_group_topology(shards);\n\n    let mut mock = MockNodeClient::default();\n\n    // Compute covering set for query_seq=0\n    let plan = plan_search_scatter(&topo, 0, 2, shards);\n\n    // Build per-node responses by accumulating all docs for each node.\n    // Multiple shards may map to the same node; a real Meilisearch node\n    // returns all its matching docs in a single response.\n    let mut node_hits: HashMap<NodeId, Vec<Value>> = HashMap::new();\n    let mut node_totals: HashMap<NodeId, u64> = HashMap::new();\n\n    for (shard_id, node_id) in &plan.shard_to_node {\n        let doc1 = json!({\n            \"id\": format!(\"doc-{}-a\", shard_id),\n            \"title\": format!(\"unique-keyword-{}\", shard_id * 2),\n            \"_miroir_shard\": shard_id,\n            \"_rankingScore\": 0.95,\n        });\n        let doc2 = json!({\n            \"id\": format!(\"doc-{}-b\", shard_id),\n            \"title\": format!(\"unique-keyword-{}\", shard_id * 2 + 1),\n            \"_miroir_shard\": shard_id,\n            \"_rankingScore\": 0.90,\n        });\n\n        let hits = node_hits.entry(node_id.clone()).or_default();\n        hits.push(doc1);\n        hits.push(doc2);\n        *node_totals.entry(node_id.clone()).or_insert(0) += 2;\n\n        mock.preflight_responses.insert(node_id.clone(), miroir_core::scatter::PreflightResponse {\n            total_docs: 100,\n            avg_doc_length: 500.0,\n            term_stats: HashMap::new(),\n        });\n    }\n\n    // Now build one response per node with all its accumulated docs\n    for (node_id, hits) in node_hits {\n        let total = node_totals.remove(&node_id).unwrap();\n        mock.responses.insert(node_id, json!({\n            \"hits\": hits,\n            \"estimatedTotalHits\": total,\n            \"processingTimeMs\": 5,\n        }));\n    }\n\n    let strategy = ScoreMergeStrategy::new();\n    let req = SearchRequest {\n        index_uid: \"test-index\".to_string(),\n        query: Some(\"unique-keyword\".to_string()),\n        offset: 0,\n        limit: 100,\n        filter: None,\n        facets: None,\n        ranking_score: false,\n        body: json!({}),\n        global_idf: None,\n    };\n\n    let result = dfs_query_then_fetch_search(\n        plan,\n        &mock,\n        req,\n        &topo,\n        miroir_core::config::UnavailableShardPolicy::Partial,\n        &strategy,\n    )\n    .await\n    .unwrap();\n\n    // Every document should appear exactly once (no duplicates)\n    let mut seen_ids: HashMap<String, usize> = HashMap::new();\n    for hit in &result.hits {\n        let id = hit[\"id\"].as_str().unwrap().to_string();\n        *seen_ids.entry(id).or_insert(0) += 1;\n    }\n\n    for (id, count) in &seen_ids {\n        assert_eq!(*count, 1, \"doc {} appeared {} times, expected 1\", id, count);\n    }\n\n    // Should find all 8 docs (2 per shard × 4 shards)\n    assert_eq!(result.hits.len(), 8, \"expected 8 unique hits across 4 shards\");\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// DoD 3: facet aggregation across 3 color values sums correctly\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n#[test]\nfn test_facet_aggregation_sums_correctly() {\n    // Test the facet merge logic directly using the same algorithm the merger uses.\n    // Since merge_facets is private, we replicate the merge logic here to validate\n    // the aggregation contract.\n    use std::collections::BTreeMap;\n\n    // Shard 0: red=10, green=5, blue=3\n    let mut shard0: BTreeMap<String, BTreeMap<String, u64>> = BTreeMap::new();\n    let mut colors0: BTreeMap<String, u64> = BTreeMap::new();\n    colors0.insert(\"red\".to_string(), 10);\n    colors0.insert(\"green\".to_string(), 5);\n    colors0.insert(\"blue\".to_string(), 3);\n    shard0.insert(\"color\".to_string(), colors0);\n\n    // Shard 1: red=7, green=12, blue=8\n    let mut shard1: BTreeMap<String, BTreeMap<String, u64>> = BTreeMap::new();\n    let mut colors1: BTreeMap<String, u64> = BTreeMap::new();\n    colors1.insert(\"red\".to_string(), 7);\n    colors1.insert(\"green\".to_string(), 12);\n    colors1.insert(\"blue\".to_string(), 8);\n    shard1.insert(\"color\".to_string(), colors1);\n\n    // Merge: sum per-value facet counts across shards\n    let mut merged: BTreeMap<String, BTreeMap<String, u64>> = BTreeMap::new();\n    for shard_facets in &[&shard0, &shard1] {\n        for (facet_name, values) in *shard_facets {\n            let entry = merged.entry(facet_name.clone()).or_default();\n            for (value, count) in values {\n                *entry.entry(value.clone()).or_insert(0) += count;\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    let colors = merged.get(\"color\").unwrap();\n    assert_eq!(*colors.get(\"red\").unwrap(), 17, \"red: 10+7=17\");\n    assert_eq!(*colors.get(\"green\").unwrap(), 17, \"green: 5+12=17\");\n    assert_eq!(*colors.get(\"blue\").unwrap(), 11, \"blue: 3+8=11\");\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// DoD 4: offset/limit paging preserves global ordering\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn test_paging_preserves_global_ordering() {\n    let shards = 3u32;\n    let topo = three_node_topology(shards);\n\n    // Each shard returns 5 hits with descending scores\n    let mut mock = MockNodeClient::default();\n\n    // Build covering set for page 1\n    let plan1 = plan_search_scatter(&topo, 0, 3, shards);\n\n    for (shard_id, node_id) in &plan1.shard_to_node {\n        let mut hits = Vec::new();\n        for i in 0..5u32 {\n            hits.push(json!({\n                \"id\": format!(\"s{}-d{}\", shard_id, i),\n                \"_miroir_shard\": shard_id,\n                \"_rankingScore\": 1.0 - (i as f64 * 0.1) - (*shard_id as f64 * 0.01),\n            }));\n        }\n        let response = json!({\n            \"hits\": hits,\n            \"estimatedTotalHits\": 5,\n            \"processingTimeMs\": 2,\n        });\n        mock.responses.insert(node_id.clone(), response);\n        mock.preflight_responses.insert(node_id.clone(), miroir_core::scatter::PreflightResponse {\n            total_docs: 50,\n            avg_doc_length: 500.0,\n            term_stats: HashMap::new(),\n        });\n    }\n\n    let strategy = ScoreMergeStrategy::new();\n\n    // Page 1: offset=0, limit=5\n    let req1 = SearchRequest {\n        index_uid: \"test\".to_string(),\n        query: Some(\"test\".to_string()),\n        offset: 0,\n        limit: 5,\n        filter: None,\n        facets: None,\n        ranking_score: true,\n        body: json!({}),\n        global_idf: None,\n    };\n    let result1 = dfs_query_then_fetch_search(\n        plan1, &mock, req1, &topo,\n        miroir_core::config::UnavailableShardPolicy::Partial, &strategy,\n    ).await.unwrap();\n\n    // Page 2: offset=5, limit=5 (different query_seq to get different covering set)\n    let plan2 = plan_search_scatter(&topo, 1, 3, shards);\n    // Re-use same mock responses since the node set is the same for this topology\n    let req2 = SearchRequest {\n        index_uid: \"test\".to_string(),\n        query: Some(\"test\".to_string()),\n        offset: 5,\n        limit: 5,\n        filter: None,\n        facets: None,\n        ranking_score: true,\n        body: json!({}),\n        global_idf: None,\n    };\n    let result2 = dfs_query_then_fetch_search(\n        plan2, &mock, req2, &topo,\n        miroir_core::config::UnavailableShardPolicy::Partial, &strategy,\n    ).await.unwrap();\n\n    // Pages should not overlap\n    let page1_ids: std::collections::HashSet<String> = result1.hits.iter()\n        .filter_map(|h| h[\"id\"].as_str().map(|s| s.to_string()))\n        .collect();\n    let page2_ids: std::collections::HashSet<String> = result2.hits.iter()\n        .filter_map(|h| h[\"id\"].as_str().map(|s| s.to_string()))\n        .collect();\n\n    let overlap: std::collections::HashSet<_> = page1_ids.intersection(&page2_ids).collect();\n    assert!(overlap.is_empty(), \"pages should not overlap, but found: {:?}\", overlap);\n\n    // Combined should have 10 hits total (5 per page)\n    assert_eq!(result1.hits.len(), 5, \"page 1 should have 5 hits\");\n    assert_eq!(result2.hits.len(), 5, \"page 2 should have 5 hits\");\n\n    // Verify global ordering: page 1 scores >= page 2 scores\n    let page1_max_score = result1.hits.last()\n        .and_then(|h| h[\"_rankingScore\"].as_f64())\n        .unwrap_or(0.0);\n    let page2_min_score = result2.hits.first()\n        .and_then(|h| h[\"_rankingScore\"].as_f64())\n        .unwrap_or(1.0);\n    assert!(\n        page1_max_score >= page2_min_score,\n        \"page 1 min score ({}) should be >= page 2 max score ({})\",\n        page1_max_score, page2_min_score\n    );\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// DoD 5: write with one group completely down still succeeds + X-Miroir-Degraded\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n#[test]\nfn test_degraded_write_one_group_down() {\n    let shards = 4u32;\n    let mut topo = two_group_topology(shards);\n\n    // Take down all nodes in group 1\n    for i in 2..4u32 {\n        topo.node_mut(&NodeId::new(format!(\"node-{i}\")))\n            .unwrap()\n            .transition_to(NodeStatus::Failed)\n            .unwrap();\n    }\n\n    // Verify group 1 nodes are not healthy\n    let node_map = topo.node_map();\n    for group in topo.groups() {\n        if group.id == 1 {\n            let healthy = group.healthy_nodes(&node_map);\n            assert!(healthy.is_empty(), \"group 1 should have no healthy nodes\");\n        }\n    }\n\n    // For each shard, verify at least one group can still accept writes\n    for shard_id in 0..shards {\n        let targets = write_targets(shard_id, &topo);\n        let group0_targets: Vec<_> = targets.iter()\n            .filter(|node_id| {\n                topo.node(node_id).map(|n| n.replica_group == 0).unwrap_or(false)\n            })\n            .collect();\n        assert!(!group0_targets.is_empty(), \"shard {} should have group 0 targets\", shard_id);\n    }\n}\n\n#[test]\nfn test_quorum_logic_group_down() {\n    // Simulate: 2 groups, RF=2. Group 1 is completely down.\n    // Quorum per group = floor(2/2) + 1 = 2.\n    // Group 0: 2 of 2 nodes succeed → quorum met.\n    // Group 1: 0 of 2 nodes succeed → quorum not met.\n    // Overall: at least one group met quorum → write succeeds, degraded header.\n\n    let rf = 2usize;\n    let replica_group_count = 2u32;\n    let quorum_per_group = (rf / 2) + 1; // = 2\n\n    // Group 0: 2 ACKs\n    let group0_acks = 2usize;\n    assert!(group0_acks >= quorum_per_group, \"group 0 met quorum\");\n\n    // Group 1: 0 ACKs\n    let group1_acks = 0usize;\n    assert!(group1_acks < quorum_per_group, \"group 1 missed quorum\");\n\n    // At least one group met quorum → write should succeed\n    let quorum_groups = (group0_acks >= quorum_per_group) as usize\n        + (group1_acks >= quorum_per_group) as usize;\n    assert!(quorum_groups >= 1, \"at least one group met quorum\");\n\n    // Degraded header should be set because group 1 missed quorum\n    let degraded_groups = replica_group_count - quorum_groups as u32;\n    assert_eq!(degraded_groups, 1, \"one group is degraded\");\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// DoD 6: error-format parity with Meilisearch\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n#[test]\nfn test_error_shape_byte_for_byte_parity() {\n    // Every MiroirCode must produce a JSON object with exactly the keys:\n    // {message, code, type, link} where code starts with \"miroir_\".\n    for code in ALL_CODES {\n        let err = MeilisearchError::new(code, format!(\"test message for {:?}\", code));\n        let json_str = serde_json::to_string(&err).unwrap();\n        let parsed: serde_json::Value = serde_json::from_str(&json_str).unwrap();\n\n        // Must have all four fields\n        assert!(parsed.get(\"message\").is_some(), \"{:?}: missing message\", code);\n        assert!(parsed.get(\"code\").is_some(), \"{:?}: missing code\", code);\n        assert!(parsed.get(\"type\").is_some(), \"{:?}: missing type\", code);\n        assert!(parsed.get(\"link\").is_some(), \"{:?}: missing link\", code);\n\n        // Code must start with miroir_\n        let code_str = parsed[\"code\"].as_str().unwrap();\n        assert!(\n            code_str.starts_with(\"miroir_\"),\n            \"{:?}: code '{}' should start with miroir_\",\n            code, code_str\n        );\n\n        // Type must be a valid Meilisearch error type\n a valid Meilisearch error type\",\n            code, type_str\n        );\n\n        // Link must be a string pointing to docs\n        let link_str = parsed[\"link\"].as_str().unwrap();\n        assert!(\n            link_str.contains(\"docs/errors.md\"),\n            \"{:?}: link '{}' should point to error docs\",\n            code, link_str\n        );\n    }\n}\n\n#[test]\nfn test_forwarded_meilisearch_error_preserves_shape() {\n    let meili_body = r#\"{\n        \"message\": \"Index `movies` not found.\",\n        \"code\": \"index_not_found\",\n        \"type\":
